ip_outcome_note: Card name is literally 'Putine, in Demigra's Thrall'. Short teal hair and magic-orb casting are Putine's signature visual identifiers from Dragon Ball Heroes. High confidence confirm.

trivia_bullets.text: Putine is a Dragon Ball Heroes original character — a skilled wizard once residing in the Heavenly Realm who was banished to the Demon Realm after Chronoa (Supreme Kai of Time) discovered her as Demigra's accomplice. She later became a Demon Goddess under Mechikabura. Uniquely among the BT4 villains, Putine eventually defects from the Dark Empire and becomes an intern Time Patroller — making her the only character on this card cluster who ends on the side of the Time Patrol. [Dragon Ball Wiki: Putine, via WebSearch]
sources: Dragon Ball Wiki: Putine, via WebSearch

trivia_bullets.text: BT4-116 is a Black Common (4000 power) in the Colossal Warfare set. It pairs directly with Gravy BT4-114 — both thralled by Demigra, both from the Dark Demon Realm Saga era. Bandai assigned them consecutive collector numbers (114, 116) reinforcing their narrative pairing. [Romulus Games product listing, via WebSearch]
sources: Romulus Games product listing, via WebSearch]

trivia_bullets.text: Putine's teal hair and orb-casting motif are her consistent visual markers across Dragon Ball Heroes media. The yellow-gold energy orb in the art matches her Heavenly Realm magic-user heritage — the orb color contrasting with the dark purple thrall energy in the background, visually signaling the tension between her original power and Demigra's imposed control. [VS Battles Wiki: Putine, via WebSearch]
sources: VS Battles Wiki: Putine, via WebSearch

trivia_bullets.text: Putine's eventual Time Patrol defection arc (which occurs after the events depicted on this card) makes her one of the more narratively complex villain-to-ally pivots in Dragon Ball Heroes lore. The thralled BT4-116 captures her at her most subjugated moment — before the redemption arc that follows in later Heroes story arcs. [Dragon Ball Wiki: Putine — TV Tropes: Dragon Ball Heroes Villains, via WebSearch]
sources: Dragon Ball Wiki: Putine, via WebSearch
TV Tropes: Dragon Ball Heroes Villains, via WebSearch
